dt-option.name: search.search
dt-option.summary: Limit the search to a specific subset of columns
dt-option.description:
It can sometimes be desirable to limit the search input for the table to a specific subset of columns. This option provides that ability.
Please note that there is some overlap with `-init columns.searchable` here - that option can be used to disable search completely for a column. This option, and the search APIs provides more nuance. For example, you might wish to have the main search input operate on a particular set of columns, but also allow a custom API search of a column not included in that set. That isn't possible with `-init columns.searchable`, but can be achieved here.
This option accepts all of the options available through `-type DataTable.ColumnSelector`. It is important to note that the selection of columns is performed when the `-feature search` feature is initialised. If DOM properties change at a later time (e.g. a class selector is used), the columns selected for this control will not be updated.
Furthermore, this feature uses shares the same methods as `-api columns().search()`. As such, while it is possible to have multiple instances of this feature, if you want them to search independently of each other, you must select a different set of columns for each control. If the control selects the same columns as another, they will be kept in-sync. Moreover, use of the `-api columns().search()` method will effect this control if the selected columns match.

example.title: Show the processing indicator when searching large data sets
